summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

actions.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a boundary audit system at src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts:97-121, 566-597 to detect project file mutations during agent execution, and converts any detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers at lines 585-597.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 hashes of all project files except allowed_write_roots, and diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after states to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit mechanism that detects file mutations

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() captures boundaryBefore snapshot before agent execution loop and boundaryAfter snapshot after all agents complete.
evidence.why: Shows boundary audit integration in execution flow

evidence.summary: Boundary diff is computed and stored in result.boundary with project_mutation flag set to 'violated' or 'clean'.
evidence.why: Boundary violation detection logic

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations are converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and added to readiness.blockers array, forcing prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Mutation-to-blocker conversion that gates PRD readiness

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary sets project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots=[output_dir] to constrain agent write permissions.
evidence.why: Boundary policy configuration

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly instructs 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and declares allowed artifact root.
evidence.why: Agent instructions that set boundary expectations

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false for all protocols.
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocol definitions

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles() returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] for cross_check policy and ['explorer', 'verifier'] for single_agent policy.
evidence.why: Evidence role requirements based on policy

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage() sets evidence_policy='cross_check' when technicalRisk or highCost signals detected, and route='careful' for hard triggers.
evidence.why: Triage logic that selects evidence policy

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() validates that evidence with project scope includes repo-relative path locators.
evidence.why: Project evidence validation that prevents unverified facts

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() executes Claude CLI with configured tools and returns structured result including stdout, stderr, exit code.
evidence.why: Agent execution mechanism that boundary audit wraps

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs Claude CLI arguments with --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools for tool profile control.
evidence.why: Tool profile configuration that affects agent capabilities

assumptions: The boundary audit system has not been stress-tested with full-tool Claude agents in a dirty working tree to verify detection accuracy.
The current working tree status may affect boundary baseline detection.
SHA-256 file digest computation at evidence-dispatch.ts:87-95 may have edge cases with symlinks, special files, or concurrent modifications.
risks: If boundary audit fails to detect mutations (false negative), PRD readiness could be granted despite project corruption.
If boundary audit produces false positives, valid evidence gathering workflows could be incorrectly blocked.
If agents execute before boundary snapshot is captured, mutations would not be detected.
If allowed_write_roots configuration is incorrect, legitimate agent writes could be flagged as violations or real violations could be missed.
missing: Integration test or manual verification that boundary audit detects mutations when full-tool Claude agents are executed in evidence dispatch.
Evidence that boundary audit correctly handles dirty working tree state where uncommitted changes exist before agent execution.
Verification that allowed_write_roots exclusion at evidence-dispatch.ts:104 correctly permits writes to .yolo/demand/evidence/* but not to other project locations.
Test coverage or evidence showing boundary behavior when agents attempt to modify files outside allowed roots.

result.notes: Explorer confirms boundary audit implementation exists at specified paths with mutation-to-blocker conversion. Independent verification recommended for stress-test scenarios and dirty working tree behavior.

agent_results.claim: Cross-check confirms explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit at src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts:97-121, 566-597, 585-597 with SHA-256 mutation detection and PRD readiness blocker conversion.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() computes SHA-256 hashes at line 91 (createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex')) and excludes allowed roots at line 104. diffBoundarySnapshots() at lines 111-121 compares before/after states.
evidence.why: Independent verification of boundary snapshot mechanism

evidence.summary: Line 516 captures boundaryBefore, line 566 captures boundaryAfter, line 567 computes diff. Confirms execution flow captured at correct locations.
evidence.why: Verification of boundary audit integration points

evidence.summary: Lines 585-590 map boundaryChanges to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers. Lines 591-597 merge blockers into finalReadiness and set prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Verification of mutation-to-blocker conversion logic

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary sets project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots=[output_dir]. Confirms boundary configuration.
evidence.why: Verification of boundary policy settings

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles() returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] for cross_check policy at line 476.
evidence.why: Verification of cross_check policy requirements

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines cross_checker with writes_code=false and required_when='evidence_policy === cross_check'.
evidence.why: Verification of cross-checker protocol definition

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() validates that project-scope evidence includes repo-relative paths at lines 514-516, 518-523.
evidence.why: Verification of project evidence path validation

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage() sets evidence_policy='cross_check' when technicalRisk or highCost are true at lines 656-658.
evidence.why: Verification of cross_check policy selection logic

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() executes child process at lines 133-137, captures stdout/stderr at lines 138-146, returns structured result at lines 183-193.
evidence.why: Verification of agent execution mechanism

evidence.summary: Claude CLI arguments include --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools for tool profile control.
evidence.why: Verification of tool configuration mechanism

risks: No independent verification exists that boundary audit correctly detects mutations in real-world scenarios with full-tool Claude agents.
SHA-256 digest computation may not handle symlinks, special files, or concurrent modifications correctly.
allowed_write_roots exclusion logic may have edge cases if output_dir paths are not properly normalized.
missing: Integration test or manual verification showing boundary audit detects mutations when full-tool Claude agents execute in evidence dispatch.
Evidence showing boundary behavior with dirty working tree (uncommitted changes) before agent execution.
Verification that allowed_write_roots at line 246 correctly excludes .yolo/demand/evidence/* from mutation detection.
Test coverage showing boundary detection when agents attempt writes outside allowed roots.

result.notes: Cross-check independently confirms all explorer claims. Line numbers accurate, code logic verified. Missing verification evidence for stress-test scenarios and dirty working tree behavior as noted in missing array.

agent_results.claim: Verifier confirms that explorer and cross-checker evidence meets verification standards: all factual claims are supported by project-scoped evidence with usable paths, assumptions are properly separated and not presented as facts, missing data is correctly reflected in missing arrays rather than promoted to evidence claims, and both agents independently verify boundary audit implementation with consistent line numbers and logic descriptions.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both cite buildBoundarySnapshot() implementation at identical line range. Independent verification confirms code exists and matches described behavior.
evidence.why: Evidence path usability verification - both agents independently located and verified identical code sections

evidence.summary: Both agents cite mutation-to-blocker conversion at identical lines 585-597. Cross-checker independently verified lines 585-590 map changes to blockers and 591-597 set prd_ready flag.
evidence.why: Confirms critical PRD readiness gating mechanism is correctly identified and independently verified

evidence.summary: Explorer cites requiredEvidenceRoles() at lines 475-479, cross-checker verifies line 476 returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] for cross_check policy. Line numbers consistent.
evidence.why: Evidence cross-agency consistency verification for policy requirements logic

evidence.summary: Both agents cite projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() validation at lines 513-530. Explorer describes path validation, cross-checker verifies specific validation at lines 514-516 and 518-523.
evidence.why: Verification that project evidence path validation logic is correctly identified

evidence.summary: Both agents cite spawnProviderPrompt() at lines 111-203. Cross-checker verified child process execution at lines 133-137 and stdout/stderr capture at 138-146.
evidence.why: Verification that agent execution mechanism evidence is independently corroborated

risks: If verifier incorrectly validates evidence paths that do not exist or are inaccessible, PRD readiness could be granted on non-verifiable claims.
If verifier fails to detect assumptions promoted as facts, demand could proceed with unverified claims as project facts.
If verifier does not confirm missing data is properly separated, critical blockers could be missed.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker provide consistent, independent verification with matching line numbers and corroborating logic descriptions. All evidence has project scope with usable paths and line locators. Assumptions are properly separated in explorer result and not presented as facts. Missing items about integration/stress testing are correctly reflected in missing arrays rather than evidence claims. Ready to proceed to PRD acceptance evaluation.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer reported unresolved missing evidence: Integration test or manual verification that boundary audit detects mutations when full-tool Claude agents are executed in evidence dispatch.; Evidence that boundary audit correctly handles dirty working tree state where uncommitted changes exist before agent execution.; Verification that allowed_write_roots exclusion at evidence-dispatch.ts:104 correctly permits writes to .yolo/demand/evidence/* but not to other project locations.; Test coverage or evidence showing boundary behavior when agents attempt to modify files outside allowed roots.

cross-checker reported unresolved missing evidence: Integration test or manual verification showing boundary audit detects mutations when full-tool Claude agents execute in evidence dispatch.; Evidence showing boundary behavior with dirty working tree (uncommitted changes) before agent execution.; Verification that allowed_write_roots at line 246 correctly excludes .yolo/demand/evidence/* from mutation detection.; Test coverage showing boundary detection when agents attempt writes outside allowed roots.
